summary: Frontend-only verification of the new Saved Card + Auto-charge feature on invoice INV-00001. All 5 requested checks PASS. Login as demo@ledgerly.app OK. On invoice detail (/invoices then click INV-00001 → routes to /invoices/{id}) the Actions panel shows saved-card-section with saved-method-select dropdown containing exactly one option 'Visa ···· 1111' and a Charge now button (charge-saved-now-btn). Because INV-00001 is monthly recurring and a saved card exists, autocharge-on-btn is rendered. Clicking it shows invoice-action-msg = 'Auto-charge enabled for this schedule' and reveals autocharge-off-btn. Clicking Turn off shows 'Auto-charge turned off' and the on-button returns. Clicking Charge now surfaces a graceful failure in invoice-action-msg: 'Cannot charge: no_saved_method' (HTTP 409 from /api/invoices/{id}/charge-saved) — this is the fail-closed guard, and the reviewer request explicitly says any clear error message is PASS. Zero JS pageerrors, zero console errors other than the expected 409 network log for the intentionally-failing charge.
critical_code_review_comments: InvoiceActions.tsx saveAutoCharge sends `savedMethodId: enabled ? selectedMethod : null` — turning off auto-charge NULLs the invoice's savedMethodId on the backend. Consequence: after Turn off, a subsequent Charge now returns 'no_saved_method' instead of the more precise 'gateway_mismatch' the request expected. Consider not nulling savedMethodId when disabling auto-charge, or make charge-saved endpoint fall back to the customer's first SavedPaymentMethod when the invoice's savedMethodId is null. Not a functional bug (guard still fires), but the surfaced error code is misleading and hides the true reason (missing gateway config).

InvoiceActions.tsx uses a single msg state for both success and errors and renders it inside `

`. Errors are shown with a success-colored style — cosmetic issue but potentially misleading (e.g., 'Cannot charge: no_saved_method' appears in a positive/success color).

Invoice list row uses non-semantic clickable container: clicking the 'Ceiling fan install' job text navigates to the parent EST-0002 estimate, but clicking 'INV-00001' navigates to the invoice detail. Two overlapping click targets in the same row is confusing UX — consider making the whole row a single link to the invoice with a distinct sub-link for the estimate.

InvoiceActions.tsx shadows the global `setInterval` name with `const [interval, setInterval] = useState(...)` — legal but risky (any accidental future use of window.setInterval inside this component will be broken). Rename to `intervalKey`/`setIntervalKey`.
seed_data_creation: None. Used seeded INV-00001 (recurring monthly) and seeded SavedPaymentMethod 'Visa ···· 1111'. Auto-charge was toggled on and then off during the test, leaving the invoice in its original state (auto-charge OFF).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test-ids verified: saved-card-section, saved-method-select, charge-saved-now-btn, autocharge-on-btn, autocharge-off-btn, invoice-action-msg. Preview URL: https://8a1fcd27-2686-4210-bc8e-4cecf5167001.preview.emergentagent.com. IMPORTANT: To reach the invoice detail page, click the 'INV-00001' text in the invoice list — clicking the job title 'Ceiling fan install' navigates to the source estimate EST-0002 instead. The observed charge-now error is 'Cannot charge: no_saved_method' (not 'gateway_mismatch') because the Turn-off action nulls the invoice's savedMethodId; both are acceptable fail-closed guards. 409 in the network tab for /api/invoices/{id}/charge-saved is EXPECTED, not a bug.
